topic: coaching highlights — a kid scored his first hat-trick

turns.content: BRUV. i have to tell someone about what happened at the game today because im still buzzing
Go on then, I can tell this is going to be good!

so one of my lads yeah. this kid has been struggling all season. works hard in training does everything you ask but in matches he just could not find the net. weve been working on his finishing for weeks and i was starting to worry about his confidence because he kept missing chances and getting in his own head. and today he scored a HAT-TRICK. three goals. first one was a tap in. second was a proper strikers finish turned the defender and slotted it far post. and the third was a volley from the edge of the box that went in off the bar. i nearly lost my voice

That's absolutely brilliant! Going from struggling in front of goal to a hat-trick capped off with a volley off the bar -- that's the kind of moment that can completely transform a young player's belief. How did he react?

thats the best part. after the third one he turned round and looked at me on the touchline with this massive grin like coach i did it. and the whole team piled on him. the other kids gave him man of the match unanimously. and they were all genuinely happy for him not jealous because they knew hed been struggling. i swear i nearly cried on the sideline but had to keep it together with the parents watching 😂

That look back at you says everything -- he wasn't just celebrating the goals, he was celebrating the work you put in together. And the team being genuinely happy for him shows the culture you've built. A lot of youth teams would have jealousy or egos. The fact yours doesn't is down to you.

man dont set me off lol. nah but honestly days like today are why i coach. tactics and formations are cool but when a kid who didnt believe in himself suddenly does thats the real win init. doesnt matter what the scoreline was. that moment is worth everything

Couldn't agree more. The scoreline gets forgotten but that kid will remember today for years. And you'll remember it too -- the volley, the grin, the team piling on. That's what makes coaching worth every cold evening. Enjoy the buzz, you've earned it.
im riding this high all week icl. alright thanks for letting me share that. needed to tell someone. have a good one bruv
Thanks for sharing it, genuinely brightened my day. Keep doing what you're doing -- that kid is lucky to have you as a coach. Have a great week!
